QUALITY Of SUCCESS After succeeding (orfailing) task; itis sometimes desirable determine exactly how well you did (or how badly you failed). common example is combat; where YOu want to know not only whether you or  missed: but also how much damage you inflicted on the target; To determine the quality 0f success or tailure, simply determine the Margin of Success (MoS) or Margin of Failure (MoF) This IS done by comparing the number rolled on the dice (including skill bonus) t0 the modified TN. For example if the TN was 13 and you rolled 15, the MoS would be because YOu exceeded what you needed roll Likewise; if you rolled an 11 your MoF would be You can uSC the Margin Success and Failure t0 determine the quality of the result of the Action Check. The Quality Success /Failure Table (p_ 18) provides descriptive values t0 the various MoSs (listed positive numbers) and MoFs (listed as negative numbers).

OPPOSED ACTION CHECKS In some cases a Action Check represents head-to-head contest  between tWO characters, such as game of chess wrestling match; Success in such case iS based on the result of the  opposing character' = Action Check. These kind of rolls are called Opposed Action Checks because they are directly opposed another character: To determine success in this case_ both char acters make Action Checks with the usual modi fiers and 50 forth; Then compare their Margins of
